_researchNotes: Edge case combining three documented realities: (1) the second OFFICIAL subject shape — article 115003720143 (Zendesk API): "An example of a new message subject line is: 'New message from John Doe'" — here with a threading 'Re:' prefix as mail clients/notification re-sends produce; (2) WeddingWire couples can mark their date flexible, so the date line carries 'Flexible' instead of a parseable date — the parser must leave eventDate unset rather than invent one; (3) follow-up message notifications are lighter than first-lead emails (the thread details live in the Messages inbox per article 115003720163), so no phone/guest lines. Client email present via Reply-To relay behaviour ('Respond directly to the client by clicking the reply button', article 115003720143). Sender pros@weddingwire.com per whitelist article 115003741366.
subject: Re: New message from Olivia Tran
textBody:
You have a new message!

Olivia Tran

Wedding Date: Flexible

Message:
Thanks for the quick reply! We haven't locked a venue yet so our wedding date is still flexible — sometime next spring. Could we set up a call this week?

Reply directly to this email to respond, or reply within your account.

The WeddingWire Team
